novel_features.name: Dashboard Sync
novel_features.description: Sync all Gravitus workouts into your training dashboard's SQLite database — writes LiftingSession records in the exact Prisma schema format with auth, pagination, and incremental support.
novel_features.rationale: The dashboard's existing /api/gravitus/sync route has no session cookie auth and no pagination, so it finds zero workouts. This CLI fixes both.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to populate the training dashboard's lifting data — the only reliable way to authenticate and paginate all workout history into dev.db.

novel_features.name: Data Export
novel_features.description: Export your complete Gravitus training history to CSV or JSON — the first and only way to get your data out of Gravitus.
novel_features.rationale: Gravitus has zero native export functionality. This CLI owns the auth layer and full paginated history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when a coach, analyst, or AI agent needs the full training history outside the app.

novel_features.name: Plateau Detection
novel_features.description: Identifies exercises where estimated 1RM hasn't improved in N weeks — alert-style output for the dashboard coaching panel.
novel_features.rationale: Requires comparing 1RM estimates across time windows from the local SQLite store — no single API call provides this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before a program change — gives evidence-based list of which lifts need intervention.

novel_features.name: Weekly Volume
novel_features.description: Weekly total lifting volume (lbs) aggregated from all synced sessions — the same metric the dashboard LiftingSection displays.
novel_features.rationale: Requires summing totalVolumeLbs across all sessions per week from the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to feed the dashboard's volume trend chart or check load progression over a training block.

novel_features.name: Personal Records
novel_features.description: All-time PRs across every exercise, extracted from PR markers on workout pages.
novel_features.rationale: Requires scanning all synced workout pages for PR markers and keeping the max per exercise — only possible with full history in SQLite.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to display the personal records panel in the dashboard or track PR cadence.

narrative.headline: The only CLI that syncs your Gravitus strength data into your training dashboard.
narrative.value_prop: Gravitus has no API and no data export. gravitus-pp-cli handles the session auth, paginates your full workout history, and writes LiftingSession records directly into your dashboard's SQLite database — incremental, reliable, and scriptable.
narrative.auth_narrative: Gravitus uses Django session auth. Run `gravitus-pp-cli auth login` with your email and password — the CLI handles the CSRF token exchange and stores your session cookie in the config file. Re-run `auth login` whenever the session expires (typically every few weeks).

quickstart.comment: authenticate with email and password — handles the CSRF dance automatically
pull all workouts into dev.db as LiftingSession records
only fetch new workouts since last sync
view all personal records as structured JSON
find lifts with no progress in 6 weeks

troubleshoots.fix: run `gravitus-pp-cli doctor` — session likely expired, re-run `auth login`
verify the path with `gravitus-pp-cli doctor --dashboard-db <path>` and ensure the file is not locked by the Next.js dev server

troubleshoots.symptom: auth login fails with CSRF error
troubleshoots.fix: run `gravitus-pp-cli auth logout` then `auth login` again to force a fresh CSRF token fetch

narrative.when_to_use: Use gravitus-pp-cli as the data pipeline for any training dashboard or analysis tool that needs Gravitus workout data. It is the only reliable way to authenticate with Gravitus, paginate full workout history, and write structured LiftingSession records into SQLite.

recipes.title: Full initial sync into dashboard
recipes.explanation: Pulls all workout pages with pagination and writes every session to dev.db

recipes.title: Incremental sync (run daily)
recipes.explanation: Only fetches workouts not already in dev.db — fast and safe to run repeatedly

recipes.title: Find plateaued lifts for coaching panel
recipes.explanation: Returns structured JSON of stalled lifts for the dashboard coaching panel

recipes.title: Export full history to CSV
recipes.explanation: Dumps every workout and set to CSV — useful for offline analysis or backup

recipes.title: Weekly volume for charting
recipes.explanation: Returns 12 weeks of volume totals as JSON — feed directly to a Recharts bar chart

trigger_phrases: load my lifting data into the dashboard
